title: Happy As The Day Is Long by Edward Taylor — Deep Poetry Lines
content:
I take the long walk up the staircase to my secret room.
Today's big news: they found Amelia Earhart's shoe, size 9.
1992: Charlie Christian is bebopping at Minton's in 1941.
Today, the Presidential primaries have failed us once again.
We'll look for our excitement elsewhere, in the last snow
that is falling, in tomorrow's Gospel Concert in Springfield.
It's a good day to be a cat and just sleep.
Or to read the Confessions of Saint Augustine.
meaning: "Happy As The Day Is Long" by Edward Taylor is a deep and nature poem consisting of 32 lines. The poet explores themes of deep and nature through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "I take the long walk up the staircase to my secret room. Today's big news: they found Amelia Earhart...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Edward Taylor's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
